creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
This just got weird(er). with the old kernel, it still has the problem. This implies that a change in userland is triggering the problem.

If I get some time, I'll bisect and try to figure out what the heck is going on here. Unfortunately, before this became obvious to me, I rebuilt he kernel, hoping that would fix the problem and blew away the Sept. 4 kernel, so I don't have the hash of the working world any longer. I'll have to start the bisect with the last Sept. 3 commit.
